title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Can Endorsement be done in Term Insurance?
A term insurance policy is normally taken for a long-term period. In fact, it is recommended that you buy the policy for the highest possible term so that you can stay covered longer for complete protection. Some term plans also offer the whole life option so that you can avail yourself of coverage up to 99 or 100 years of age. These long-term plans allow financial security and help your family financially in your absence.
While term plans can run for long-term periods, your requirements or needs might change during the coverage tenure. Can you change your term plan according to your changing needs?
Actually, you can. There are some aspects of the term plan that can be changed through endorsement. Let’s understand.
What is an endorsement in insurance?
Endorsement is the process of making changes or alterations in your insurance policy. So, if you want to make changes in your life insurance policy and such changes are allowed by the life insurance company, you can record the changes through an endorsement in your life insurance policy. After the insurance policy endorsement, the changed terms become a part of the policy and hold relevance till maturity or early death.
Can endorsement be done in a term insurance policy?
Yes, a term insurance policy, like other life insurance plans, offers the endorsement facility. You can make changes to your policy during the coverage duration.
To make the endorsement, you just have to submit a request to the insurance company that states the change that you need. Depending on the change that you seek, the insurance company might ask for an additional premium or require medical tests before allowing the change.
Changes during the free look period
After you buy the policy, insurance companies allow a free look period of 15 days or 30 days. This period starts from the policy issue date. During the free look period, you can cancel the policy and seek a refund of your premium.
Life insurance policies allow you to make changes to your policy even during the free look period. You can submit a request to the insurer for the changes that you want in your policy. The insurer would record the changes and make an insurance policy endorsement.
Types of endorsements in term insurance
There are different types of endorsements that you can do in your term insurance plan. These include the following –
Change in your personal details:
Changes in your mobile number, email ID or communication address can be updated in the policy through an endorsement. Moreover, if you change your name during the policy tenure, you can get that recorded in your policy. However, for such a change, you would have to submit proof of name change.
Change in nominee details:
The nominee is the one who claims the death benefit when the insured passes away. A nominee, once appointed, can also be changed during the policy tenure. So, if you want to name another nominee or add a nominee to the policy and split the benefit amount, you can do so through an endorsement.
Assignment of the policy:
Assignment means transferring the ownership of the policy to another individual or entity. When you do so, the title of the policy changes; however, the life insured remains the same.
The assignment is usually done if you avail of a loan against the policy. In such cases, you assign the policy to the lender’s name and get funds.
If you make an assignment, the details of the assignment are recorded in the policy through an endorsement.
Age correction:
If you have recorded the wrong age at the time of buying the policy and want to correct it, later on, you can do so through endorsements. To correct the recorded age, submit valid age proof and submit to the insurer with a request for the same.
Addition or removal of riders:
Riders are optional coverage features that you can add to your term insurance plan. These benefits can be added either when you buy the policy or when you renew it. When you add the rider at the time of buying the policy, it gets recorded in the policy at the same time. However, if you add the rider on a renewal, you need to endorse the same in the policy.
Alternatively, if you remove any chosen rider on renewals, the same would be done through an endorsement.
Change in the sum assured:
Any increase or decrease in the sum assured is also done through an endorsement in the policy. This increase or decrease is allowed on renewals, and the premium is also changed accordingly.
Change in term:
In some cases, you might be allowed to change the policy tenure and/or the premium payment term. Such changes are recorded in the policy with the help of an endorsement. Even if you change the premium paying frequency from annual to half-yearly, quarterly or monthly, or vice-versa, the change is allowed through an endorsement.
Exercise of the conversion option:
Some term plans allow the conversion option wherein you can convert the plan to an endowment policy. If the conversion option is allowed and you choose it, the plan will be endorsed.
Conclusion
There is a scope for making changes in your term insurance policy during the policy tenure. You can make alterations and get them recorded as a part of your policy through endorsements. Request the insurer for the change and get the policy endorsed. The endorsement might include a change in your premium, fresh underwriting or can be done without either. Find out the changes allowed and then opt for them if required.
